name: Turkey with Oyster-Cornbread Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Wipe the turkey well with a damp cloth, inside and out.
2. Remove any pinfeathers and singe off hairs.
3. Simmer the giblets in 1 1/2 cups of water for 20 to 30 minutes. Remove from cooking water and chop. Save the cooking liquid.
4. Mix all dressing ingredients together thoroughly and stuff both neck and body cavities of the bird. Wrap remaining stuffing in aluminum foil.
5. Place the turkey breast down on a poultry rack in a large roasting pan. Put the foil-wrapped stuffing at the bottom of the pan.
6. Rub the bird generously with about 1/4 cup of softened butter.
7. Roast the turkey, uncovered, in a moderately slow oven at 325°F, basting every 20 minutes with a mixture of oyster liquid, giblet-cooking water, and remaining melted butter.
8. After 1 1/2 hours of roasting, turn the turkey breast side up. Allow about 30 minutes per pound for further roasting until the leg joint moves easily.
9. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
